title: Failed to delete columns of type subitem
excerpt: Can the sub-item type column no longer be excluded from the board? I think this takes away some of the dynamism. I accidentally put it in a painting, and I had to redo it. Could you guys rethink this?

posts.author: franklin.oliveira (Franklin da silva oliveira)
posts.cookedHtml: Can the sub-item type column no longer be excluded from the board? I think this takes away some of the dynamism. I accidentally put it in a painting, and I had to redo it. Could you guys rethink this?

posts.author: mw01 (MW)
posts.cookedHtml: Wondering same thing. This is really frustrating.

posts.author: Dani (Dani Lester)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @franklin.oliveira and @mw01

At this time, it’s no longer possible to delete the subitems column from a board after it has been added. This limitation was put in place after several users experienced issues when deleting this column. While I know it may seem like a constraint, the change was implemented with the aim to eliminate any bad user experience.

Moving forward, if you do not wish to have the subitem column:

  • You can collapse it


​​

  • Or Hide it

​

If you would like to use a different subitem column, at this time, you will not able able to do so.

I understand this might not be quite convenient for your workflow, and it definitely is a change. However, this is directly connected to the steps which lead to product improvements moving forward.

As always, you can keep up with the roadmap for subitems here and if you would like to provide feedback on this change, feel free to use the form here.

I hope this helps! Remember to mark as solved if this answers your question

Best,

Dani

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
